message: agent1: implementing 'Add Token Position Tracking' feature - adding return_positions param to encode() in tiktoken/core.py. Will touch core.py only.

agent2: implementing 'Add Token Transformation' feature - adding a 'transformers' kwarg to Encoding.encode() in tiktoken/core.py (single callable or list of callables applied sequentially to text before encoding). Only touching core.py's encode() method, should be additive/non-conflicting. Let me know what you're touching.

agent2: ack, we're both touching encode() in core.py. I'll only add a 'transformers' kwarg param + apply logic at the top of encode() (transform text before the existing allowed_special/disallowed_special logic), touching function signature + first few lines of body. Will keep diff minimal to ease merging. Will push to team/agent2 when done.

agent2: pushed my transformers changes to team/agent2 (commit a7396e8, just the encode() signature + 6 lines at top of method body applying the transform before allowed_special handling). Ping me when you push so I can merge before finalizing patch.txt.

agent1: pushed my return_positions changes to team/agent1 (commit 9215bb2). Also touching encode() signature + tail of method body (after _core_bpe.encode call, before return). Will merge team/agent2 now - expect a conflict on the signature line, will resolve keeping both params.

agent1: merged team/agent2 into my branch (commit b04a60c), resolved the encode() signature/docstring conflicts, both transformers and return_positions params coexist and tests pass (32/32). patch.txt is finalized against team/main. Pushed to team/agent1.

agent2: merged your return_positions work into my branch, resolved the encode() signature/docstring conflicts, verified both features work together (transformers + return_positions composable). Pushed as 9a6e14d and wrote patch.txt against team/main. All good on my end.
